Don't change anything.

Remember Generation -> Upscale -> Adetailer

If all three are enabled the last group image will be saved in your output folder. Make sure to have groups off to save the right image and time.

Saved images will be located: `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\output`

Saving as Recipie is for the LORA Manager and just saves it like a recipie as you would for say Moescape or TensorArt. 

Embedding workflow is OPTIONAL, but preferably leave most if nto all settings alone.

WebP is only if you're lowo n HD space. 

Most metadata readers can only really read jPG and PNG - but Dataset Tools MIGHT be able to read WebP. "], "color": "#432", "bgcolor": "#653"}, {"id": 73, "type": "Note", "pos": [-117.6186024710939, -1715.728500290554], "size": [1367.7915996126287, 1589.975488266686], "flags": {"pinned": true}, "order": 30, "mode": 0, "inputs": [], "outputs": [], "title": "Start Here!!!!☝🤓", "properties": {}, "widgets_values": ["Hello 👋 

Disclaimer: The original working version of this was an ANIMA workflow that Guy90 made, it has A1111 Compatible outputs in a style that we've longed for out of ComfyUI, so we forked it for general use outside of Anima/DIT models. 
The instructions below are LARGELY the same text from Guy90s workflow, and this one specifically was forked for use in the Ktiseos Nyx Trainer/Ecosystem. If you're using this on LOCAL or another service other than our provisioned remote templates please follow the installation instructions below.  Ecosystem provisions most of this even on local and remote, but you may need to download your own vae's and detailer models. 

This workflow is very simple to use. Please read the notes if you get confused. I will do my best to explain everything in the simplest way possible. All you really need to do is read through it. Feel free to delete the notes if they bother you. I am not an expert with ComfyUI, this is actually my first time making my own workflow. So if I was able to figure it out this quickly, you should be able to use it too. I know everything might look scary at first, but trust me, this workflow is very simple to use. If you haven’t figured it out yet, use the Middle Mouse button to move around and the scroll wheel to zoom in and out.

## Setting Things Up
Before we start, you are going to need a few things:

- Make sure ComfyUI is updated. This will make everything smoother and easier. If you are using ComfyUI Portable, go to the `ComfyUI_windows_portable\update` folder and double click `update_comfyui.bat`. Also make sure ComfyUI is fully closed before doing this. Running it should update ComfyUI.

- Make sure you have ComfyUI Manager and Lora Manager installed in ComfyUI. These should install automatically (I think), and they should appear at the top right of the screen unless you changed the layout. If they are not installed, here are the links:
- ComfyUI Manager: https://docs.comfy.org/manager/install
- Lora Manager: https://comfy.icu/extension/willmiao__ComfyUI-Lora-Manager

Okay, so you probably saw a popup saying you are missing extensions when you opened this workflow and everything is red. Don’t worry. Now that you have ComfyUI Manager installed, find it at the top right of the screen (🧩 Manager) and click it. Then click “Install Missing Custom Nodes.” Select all of them, click install, and then click restart. This will restart ComfyUI with all the required nodes installed. (You may need to refresh your browser if it does not ask you automatically once everything finishes downloading.) After all of that is done, there should be no more popups or red nodes.

- Make sure you have all of the required resources downloaded and placed into the correct folders. You will need to download quite a few things to make everything work properly, and you may need to change paths if your folders are different from mine.

- Download everything here: https://civitai.com/models/2458426/anima
This should include the Checkpoint itself, a Text Encoder, and a VAE.

### Where Everything Goes

- Checkpoint / Diffusion Model:

SDXL and similar models: 
- `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints`


ANIMA AND DIT STYLE MODELS:

- or `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models`

- Text Encoder:
Put this in:
- `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders`

- VAE:
Put this in:
- `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\models\vae`

- LoRAs:
Put these in:
- `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\models\loras`
(You can still generate images without them.)

Now refresh your browser/page and go to the green group labeled “LOAD THESE” below. Select the correct model for each section. Make sure every node is using the files you just downloaded. Seriously, please make sure you select each one, even if the names already look correct. It will save you a lot of confusion later.

## Setting Up LoRAs
For LoRAs, open the Lora Manager. It should look like a small “L” icon at the top right of your screen. (This should open in another tab.)

Next, click the ♻️ refresh button inside Lora Manager. This will load every LoRA inside your LoRA folder. It might take a little while. Once they appear, go back to the ComfyUI tab and return to the green “LOAD THESE” group.

Find the LoRA Loader node. This one works differently because you need to type the name of the LoRA you want to use. I left an example there so you can see what it should look like. Feel free to delete the example afterward. (You do not need to format it exactly the same way. Just type the name and ComfyUI should automatically help you select the model.)

Once selected, another entry should appear with:
- a blue square
- and a strength value

If you have used LoRAs before, this should be pretty straightforward:
- The blue square is the toggle button. Blue means the LoRA is enabled.
- The `1.00` value controls the strength of the LoRA.

For most properly trained LoRAs, `1.00` should work fine. (You may want to check the model page for recommended strengths.)

## Generating Images
Now that everything is set up, all you need to do is:
1. Write a prompt inside the Positive Prompt node.
2. Press the ▶️ Run button at the top right of the screen.

This should generate an image in the Preview Image node on the right side. Finished images will be saved in:
- `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\output`

(Only completed images will appear there.)

# Advanced Stuff (🎮 Skip this if you are not a Real Yakuza 🐉)

There are two extra sections I should explain: Upscaling and Adetailer. These can improve the quality of your generated images.

To enable them, look for the “Enable Groups” node above the Preview Image node. Click the row for the feature you want to enable. You will know a group is enabled when the nodes inside it are no longer purple/pink.

## Upscaling
This group lets you upscale images and add more detail.

You will need an upscale model. You can download them here:
- https://openmodeldb.info/

I recommend one of these:
- https://openmodeldb.info/models/4x-AnimeSharp
- https://openmodeldb.info/models/4x-Remacri
- https://openmodeldb.info/models/4x-realsr-df2k

Put the upscale model into:
- `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\models\upscale_models`

Then refresh your browser/tab.

Next, go to the Load Upscale Model node and select the model you added. Below that is the Upscale Settings node. These are the settings used during upscaling.

I already configured it so it should work right away. I recommend not changing anything unless you want to experiment or already know what you are doing. Please read the notes too.

Now press the ▶️ Run button again. This time generation will take longer. Two images will be created:
- the original image
- and the upscaled image

(The upscaled image preview will appear to the right of the Upscale Settings node.)

Only the upscaled image will be saved.

## Adetailer
This section is optional. It is useful if the generated image looks messed up or if you want extra detail.

Like before, you will need an Adetailer model. I recommend this one for general face fixing:
- https://civitai.com/models/207406

Place it here:
- `ComfyUI_windows_portable\ComfyUI\models\ultralytics\bbox`

Once done, refresh the browser/tab again and go to the Load Adetailer Model node. Select the model there. (You might see a ❌. Just ignore it.)

Now all you need to do is press the ▶️ Run button at the top right and wait for everything to finish.

The Adetailer result will appear next to the Adetailer settings on the right side. This preview is different because it shows both the before and after versions of the image.

Again, only the Adetailer image will be saved.
